CFF fixtures — source and licensing
====================================

Four small CFF byte streams, extracted from upstream OpenType
font programs and redistributed here under the SIL Open Font
License v1.1 (see OFL.txt) or the Adobe-OFL Source Han license.
Chosen for diverse coverage of the CFF v1 feature surface:

  * non-CID (Type-1-style CFF) and CID-keyed CFF
  * different unitsPerEm-default uses (all default to 1000)
  * different numbers of subroutines and contour shapes
  * presence of cubic close-paths (real geometry) AND
    line-only close-paths (no control points)

Fixtures
--------

  masaramgondi.cff      — extracted from Noto Sans Masaram
                          Gondi-Regular (8 glyphs subset).
                          Non-CID. Upstream:
                          notofonts.github.io / noto-fonts
                          Source font copied from the macOS
                          system font library (/System/Library/
                          Fonts/Supplemental/), which
                          redistributes Google's official Noto
                          build under SIL OFL.

  yezidi.cff            — extracted from Noto Serif Yezidi-
                          Regular (6 glyphs subset). Non-CID,
                          contains script-specific complex
                          curves. Upstream + redistribution
                          path same as masaramgondi.

  tifinagh.cff          — extracted from Noto Sans Tifinagh-
                          Regular (7 glyphs subset). Non-CID,
                          square-and-circle script with mixed
                          straight + cubic-curve closepaths.
                          Upstream + redistribution path same
                          as masaramgondi.

  sourcehansans_cn.cff  — extracted from Source Han Sans CN
                          Light (8 glyphs subset). CID-keyed
                          (FDArray + FDSelect). Upstream:
                          adobe-fonts/source-han-sans on
                          GitHub, Adobe-OFL.

The .cff fixture files are committed; the source OTFs are not
(some are >5 MB). Re-derive with `python3 make_corpus.py` —
that script subsets the source fonts and writes the .cff
files. Re-run `python3 generate_fixtures.py` afterwards to
materialise the .canonical sidecars.

License notes
-------------

SIL Open Font License v1.1 — see OFL.txt for the full text.
Permits redistribution and modification of the font (or
extracts thereof, like our .cff slices) provided:

  * the font name is preserved or modified consistently,
  * derivatives are released under the same OFL,
  * the font is not sold by itself.

Source Han Sans is licensed under the SIL OFL by Adobe. The
8-glyph subset preserves Adobe's font-name strings inside the
CFF Name INDEX (so the font's identity remains attributable)
and is distributed under the same OFL terms.
